CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-16-2018 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-26-2018 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On May-01-18 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS NANCY LEE, NAM SHI LEE, THE LEE REVOCABLE TRUST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Appearances: Anne C. Gritzer of Walsworth (415.544.7142) for defendants and moving parties; Melanie D. Popper of the Revelatory Center for Arts and the Law (800.678.8476) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant's argument that Defendant is entitled to a summary judgment on Plaintiff's complaint as a matter of law because plaintiffs are unable to establish that Defendants breached any duty (Motion 1:5-28; 2:16-9:1) does not dispose of all causes of action in the First Amended Complaint. Motion for Summary Adjudication on issues 1 to 4 is granted. Defendants shifted their burden and Plaintiff failed to create a material triable issue of fact on any of the issues presented. As to the first issue, Plaintiff fails to present any evidence that the landlord was in violation of CCP 1942.4, specifically that the landlord demanded/collected rent while a notification of habitability issues made by a public officer was outstanding for over 35 days. As to the second issue, Plaintiff argues that there is a triable issue of fact as to when Plaintiff knew of should have known that the mold was a substantial factor in causing her bronchial infection, no evidence is cited to indicate that the date of accrual was later than July 2013. The Court notes that opposition as to issues 3 and 4 is limited to 5 lines each and is devoid of any citations to facts or authority. The Court further notes that Plaintiffs do not assert that an affidavit required under CCP 377.32 has been filed. CCP 437c(h) request is DENIED. Plaintiff fails to identify what essential facts pertaining to issues 1-4 may be obtained at Defendant's opposition and why those facts have not been timely obtained. Court and counsel discuss objections. Counsel are admonished on the issue of following Court orders. Order signed. Judge: Ronald Quidachay, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/REQ)
